NEWS BJJ ARTICLES SHOP CONTACT CONTRIBUTE AN ARTICLE PRIVACY POLICY HomeArticlesBJJ NewsArman Tsarukyan: “I’m More Well-Rounded Than Prime Khabib” Arman Tsarukyan: “I’m More Well-Rounded Than Prime Khabib” APR 08, 2026BJJEE1 AdminArticles, BJJ News Arman Tsarukyan continues to stay active outside the octagon while waiting for his next UFC fight. Speaking in an interview with Hassan Yosufi, Tsarukyan didn’t shy away when asked how he would fare against a prime Khabib Nurmagomedov: It’s probably a decision… My skills [are my biggest advantage]. I’m more well-rounded than him. That’s a significant statement considering Nurmagomedov’s legacy. Khabib retired in 2020 with a perfect 29-0 record, finishing elite opponents like Conor McGregor, Dustin Poirier, and Justin Gaethje in his final run. Despite that dominance, Tsarukyan believes his overall skill set gives him the edge, pointing to his evolution as a complete mixed martial artist. Interestingly, Nurmagomedov himself has previously spoken highly of Tsarukyan. Back in 2025, he described him as a more competitive matchup for Islam Makhachev than Ilia Topuria, calling Tsarukyan “a more serious fighter”. Tsarukyan’s confidence is backed by results. Since 2022, he has won five consecutive MMA bouts, including a split decision victory over former champion Charles Oliveira and a second-round submission win against Dan Hooker in late 2025. He’s also remained highly active in grappling competition, continuing to sharpen his skills across different formats. His next appearance is scheduled at Real American Freestyle 8 on April 18, where he is set to face Urijah Faber. Sloth Jiu-Jitsu: The Ultimate Guide for Calmly Beating Bigger, Younger Opponents 💥 What if slowing down was the fastest way to win? 💭 Tired of Getting Smashed by Younger, Stronger Grapplers? It’s not your age. It’s your strategy. If you’re a passionate grappler over 35 who’s sick of getting outpaced, outmuscled, or out-cardio’d by opponents half your age — this 120 page e-book was made for you. 🔓 Unlock the Power of Sloth Jiu-Jitsu: Build Sustainable Strength—Without Wrecking Your Joints, Energy, or Jiu-Jitsu Tired of waking up sore and burnt out after lifting? Sloth Strength is a proven blueprint for grapplers over 35 who want to get stronger—without the pain, burnout, or injuries. Developed from 25 years on the mats, this no-burnout approach is made specifically for BJJ athletes in their 30s, 40s, and beyond.
